name: cgen

metadata.name: cgen
metadata.summary: C/C++ source generation from an AST

documentation:
# cgen: An Abstract Syntax Tree for C, in Python

```{image} https://gitlab.tiker.net/inducer/cgen/badges/master/pipeline.svg
:alt: Gitlab Build Status
:target: https://gitlab.tiker.net/inducer/cgen/commits/master
```

```{image} https://dev.azure.com/ak-spam/inducer/_apis/build/status/inducer.cgen?branchName=master
:alt: Azure Build Status
:target: https://dev.azure.com/ak-spam/inducer/_build/latest?definitionId=7&branchName=master
```

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/cgen.png
:alt: Python Package Index Release Page
:target: https://pypi.org/project/cgen/
```

cgen offers a simple abstract syntax tree for C and related languages
(C++/CUDA/OpenCL) to allow structured code generation from Python.
To represent mathematical expressions, cgen can be used with [pymbolic](https://github.com/inducer/pymbolic).

Places on the web related to cgen:

- [Python package index](http://pypi.python.org/pypi/cgen) (download releases)

  ```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/cgen.png
  :target: http://pypi.python.org/pypi/cgen
  ```

- [Documentation](http://documen.tician.de/cgen) (read how things work)

- [Github](http://github.com/inducer/cgen) (get latest source code, file bugs)

cgen is licensed under the liberal [MIT license](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MIT_License) and free for commercial, academic,
and private use. All of cgen's dependencies can be automatically installed from
the package index after using:

```
pip install cgen
```

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: doc.source.conf
documentation:
Failed to index ``doc/source/conf.py`` due to ``Syntax Error @ 199:37.
parser error: error at 198:46: expected one of !=, %, &, (, ), *, **, +, ,, -, ., /, //, :=, <, <<, <=, ==, >, >=, >>, @, [, ^, and, if, in, is, not, or, |

   ur'Andreas Kloeckner', 'manual'),
                                    ^``

modules.name: cgen.mapper
documentation: Generator for C/C++.

methods.name: cgen.mapper.ASTMapper.__call__
documentation:
Dispatch *node* to its corresponding mapper method. Pass on
`*args` and `**kwargs` unmodified.

This method is intended as the top-level dispatch entry point and may
be overridden by subclasses to present a different/more convenient
interface. {meth}`rec` on the other hand is intended as the recursive
dispatch method to be used to recurse within mapper method
implementations.

methods.name: cgen.mapper.ASTMapper.handle_unsupported
documentation:
Mapper method that is invoked for node subclasses for which a mapper
method does not exist in this mapper.

methods.name: cgen.mapper.IdentityMapper.map_ispc_varying
documentation: {{{ ispc

methods.name: cgen.mapper.IdentityMapper.map_cuda_global
documentation: {{{ cuda

modules.name: cgen
documentation: Generator for C/C++.

methods.name: cgen.Generable.__str__
documentation:
Return a single string (possibly containing newlines) representing
this code construct.

methods.name: cgen.Generable.generate
documentation: Generate (i.e. yield) the lines making up this code construct.

classes.name: cgen.Declarator

methods.name: cgen.Declarator.get_decl_pair
documentation:
Return a tuple `(type_lines, rhs)`.

*type_lines* is a non-empty list of lines (most often just a
single one) describing the type of this declarator. *rhs* is the right-
hand side that actually contains the function/array/constness notation
making up the bulk of the declarator syntax.

methods.name: cgen.Declarator.inline
documentation: Return the declarator as a single line.
{{{ declarators

classes.name: cgen.POD
documentation:
A simple declarator: The type is given as a {class}`numpy.dtype`
and the *name* is given as a string.

classes.name: cgen.Value
documentation: A simple declarator: *typename* and *name* are given as strings.

classes.name: cgen.Struct

methods.name: cgen.Struct.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the structure declarator.
*tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
*fields* is a list of {class}`Declarator` instances.
A structure declarator.
{{{ struct-like

methods.name: cgen.GenerableStruct.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a structure declarator.
*tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
*fields* is a list of {class}`Declarator` instances.

*align_bytes* is an integer that causes the structure to be
padded to an integer multiple of itself.
*aligned_prime_to* is a list of integers.
If the resulting structure's size
is `s`, then `s//align_bytes` will be made prime to all
numbers in *aligned_prime_to*. (Sounds obscure? It's needed
for avoiding bank conflicts in CUDA programming.)

methods.name: cgen.GenerableStruct.make
documentation:
Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
{class}`str` instance with members set to the values specified
in *kwargs*.

methods.name: cgen.GenerableStruct.make_with_defaults
documentation:
Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
{class}`str` instance with members set to the values specified
in *kwargs*.

Unlike {meth}`make`, not all members have to occur in *kwargs*.

methods.name: cgen.GenerableStruct.struct_format
documentation:
Return the format of the struct as digested by the {mod}`struct`
module.

methods.name: cgen.GenerableStruct.__len__
documentation: Return the number of bytes occupied by this struct.

classes.name: cgen.Enum

methods.name: cgen.Enum.get_c_defines
documentation: Return a string with C defines corresponding to these constants.

methods.name: cgen.Enum.get_c_typedef_line
documentation: Returns a typedef to define this enum in C.

An enum-like class for Python that can generate an equivalent C-level
declaration. Does not work within the usual 'declarator' framework
because it uses macros to define values, and a separate typedef to define the
value type.

:::{versionadded} 2013.2
:::

To use, derive from this class, and define the following things:

```
.. attribute:: c_name
```

```
.. attribute:: dtype
```

```
.. attribute:: c_value_prefix

    A (usually upper-case) prefix to be used as a prefix to the names defined
    on the Python side.
```

```
.. attribute:: VALUE

    Any class attribute with an all-upper-case name is taken to be as an
    enum value.
```

classes.name: cgen.InlineInitializer
documentation:
Class to represent Initializers (int i=0) without the semicolon in the end
(e.g. in a for statement)
Usage: same as cgen.Initializer
Result: same as cgen.Initializer except for the lack of a semi-colon at the end

methods.name: cgen.FunctionBody.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a function definition. *fdecl* is expected to be
a {class}`FunctionDeclaration` instance, while *body* is a
{class}`Block`.

classes.name: cgen.Block
documentation: {{{ block

classes.name: cgen.IfDef
documentation:
Class to represent IfDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
\:param condition: the condition in IfDef
\:param iflines: the block of code inside the if [an array of type Generable]
\:param elselines: the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]

classes.name: cgen.IfNDef
documentation:
Class to represent IfNDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
\:param condition: the condition in IfNDef
\:param ifndeflines: the block of code inside the if not

> [an array of type Generable]

```
param elselines

the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]
```
